title: Rearing of Drosophila on Holidic Media (HM) for feeding behavior assays

steps: Prepare yeast-based medium (YBM) according to the Guidelines . YBM can be stored at 18ºC up to 3 days before use.
Set up fly cultures using 5 females and 4 males per YBM vial to ensure a homogenous density of offspring among experiments.

Keep the crosses in a dedicated incubator at 25º C, 70% relative humidity, and a 12-hr-light-dark cycle. Remove parental flies after 3 to 7 days and wait 14 days (since the day the crosses were set up) to obtain adult flies. If you are using temperature sensitive alleles adjust rearing temperature accordingly and plan your experiments to account for the delay in the development.

Sort the progeny according to the desired genotype, and collect 16 females into fresh YBM. Add 5 wild-type males to ensure that the females are mated. When testing males collect 20 males into fresh YBM.
To ensure a well-fed state and minimize the microbial load, transfer the flies to fresh YBM after 48 hours.

After 24 hours on fresh YBM tansfer the flies to the different HM. For this, prepare all the different HM needed according to the Holidic media (HM) preparation protocol. If required, innoculate HM with the commensal bacteria following the Inoculation of Holidic Media (HM) with bacteria protocol.
Keep the flies on HM (with or withouth commensal bacteria) for 72 hours, and immediately test for feeding behavior. Flies treated using this protocol usually have a very low internal bacterial load.
If you wish to manipulate the flies' microbiome start the liquid bacterial cultures following the protocol Growing Drosophila gut bacteria .
creator: Carlos Ribeiro
